pattern: Storing the dictionary words reversed in a trie turns suffix matching of the incoming stream into a single prefix walk over the most recent characters.
hints: You are asked about suffixes of the stream, and a suffix read backward is a prefix; reversing the words makes this a prefix problem.
Build a trie of all words spelled backward; then a query walks the recent stream characters from newest to oldest down that trie.
Keep the stream's recent characters and, on each query, descend the reversed-word trie newest-first, reporting true as soon as you pass through any end-of-word node.

edge_inputs.description: two- and one-letter words detected as the stream advances
a single one-letter word matches immediately
a word completes only when its full suffix is present
overlapping words where a longer match follows a shorter one
repeated characters that re-trigger the same word

stress.description: random dictionary and random stream over a small alphabet so matches occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    alpha = 'abc'
    nwords = rng.randint(1, 8)
    words = []
    for _ in range(nwords):
        wl = rng.randint(1, 4)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(wl)))
    ops = ['StreamChecker']
    args = [[words]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        ops.append('query'); args.append([rng.choice(alpha)])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: small alphabet and short words guarantee frequent true results
